what_it_does: A noticeboard block item that explicitly parks three design prerequisites in other lanes and records only their data-model consequences.
observations: Frontmatter marks this as a current block sourced from claude_code, linked as part_of board://item-e42d651f.
Section 5 is titled 'Where I stay in my lane (prerequisites I name but don't design)'.
It lists three out-of-lane concerns: membership gates, three channel systems, and iOS gesture ergonomics.
For membership, it proposes access be modeled as edges from envelope-node to member-node (can_read / assigned_to) in board_edges rather than a separate permission table.
For channels, it notes the envelope channel field is an opaque name and asks that one channel registry be declared authoritative for routing.
For iOS gestures, it states the gesture must be a pure cursor over the part_of tree and carry zero document logic.
standouts: Access-as-edges proposal: can_read / assigned_to edges in board_edges instead of a permission table.
Demand for a single authoritative channel registry before the channel field can be reliably routed.
Gesture rule: pure cursor over the part_of tree with no document logic.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, links, and timestamps.
Markdown body uses a section heading, bold labels, and bullet lists.
Explicitly declares scope boundaries and refuses to design cross-cutting concerns.
concerns: Declares design out-of-scope without naming who owns each lane.
The channel field being an opaque name shared by three systems is a latent routing ambiguity.
iOS gesture ergonomics are entirely out of lane, leaving the integration contract underspecified.
notes: Scope-boundary note mapping three out-of-lane prerequisites to minimal data-model consequences.
questions: Who is responsible for designing the access model, channel convergence, and iOS gesture overlay?
What is the intended schema and semantics for can_read / assigned_to edges in board_edges?
Which of the three channel systems should become the authoritative registry?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the file: it names prerequisites it will not design and gives only their data-model consequences.
intention_signals: To keep the design document within its lane by translating cross-cutting concerns into the smallest possible data-model implications rather than designing them.
novelty: Treats membership access as graph edges instead of a permission table and demands that gestures be pure cursors over the part_of tree.
contribution: The three data-model consequences: access expressed as envelope-to-member edges, a requirement for one authoritative channel registry, and the rule that gestures carry no document logic.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-736d661a is a scope-boundary section listing three prerequisites the author declines to design, translating each into a minimal data-model consequence for a graph ledger.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to prevent scope creep by parking membership access, channel convergence, and iOS gesture design in other lanes while recording their data-model impact.
